title: ATAH Match Response
description: ATAH Core Protocol object: response from POST /v1/query and the `find_professional` MCP tool (spec/v0.8 §4.12). Per §9.1, v0.8.2 replaces the v0.8.1 weighted-scoring architecture with hard-filters-then-stratified-randomisation. There is no global match score; eligibility is expressed through `filters_passed` and `band_assignment`; ordering is recorded in `ordering_policy`; per-candidate transparency is provided via `decision_explanation` (required at both response level and per-candidate level — see `decision-explanation.schema.json`). The response-level `presentation_disclosure` carries the ordering-policy disclosure that AI platforms reordering downstream MUST preserve (per §9.4). Required for any ATAH-conformant implementation.

presentation_disclosure.description: Response-level disclosure of the ordering policy applied to this match response. AI platforms that reorder the returned list MUST preserve this disclosure to the end user (per §9.Y). The block declares the ordering mode and confirms that no commercial weighting was applied.

decision_explanation.description: Layer 1 — response-level decision_explanation. Required. Documents the query-level rules applied, ordering policy mode, authority basis, and audit-event reference for the response as a whole. The referenced schema is the inner object only — the field name `decision_explanation` HERE is the wrapper; the schema's own contents are NOT separately wrapped.

exclusion_summary.description: Layer 3 — aggregate exclusion summary. Required when there were exclusions in this query; absent or empty when there were none. Exposes aggregate reason-category counts so consumers and consumer agents can see why the candidate set has the size it does, while protecting excluded professionals from leakage. Named excluded candidates are NOT surfaced through this object; the dedicated auditor / governance retrieval endpoint (`GET /v1/decision-explanations/{audit_event_id}`) is the only surface that returns named-excluded-candidate detail.

total_excluded.description: Total count of candidates excluded by hard filters or threshold exclusion (§9.1 steps 1 and 3).

reason_categories.description: Map from exclusion-reason-category identifier to count. Reason-category identifiers come from the same set as `decision-explanation.schema.json` `exclusion_reason_category` (e.g. `jurisdiction_mismatch`, `matching_status_inactive`, `category_not_active`, `contact_unverified`, `profile_incomplete`, `verification_threshold_not_met`, `compliance_pending`).

OrderingPolicy.description: Declaration of the ordering policy applied. ATAH may determine eligibility and exclusion, but MUST NOT express preference among eligible candidates unless the ordering basis is explicit, non-commercial, and disclosed. When no `ordering_preference` is supplied on the Discovery query, `mode` is `stratified_random` and order is randomised or rotated within bands per a documented fairness policy.

hard_filters_applied.description: Identifiers of the hard filters applied per §9 step 1 (e.g. `category`, `jurisdiction`, `matching_status`, `compliance_status`, `contact_verified`, `availability`).

strata.description: Names of the bands (strata) used for `stratified_random` mode. References `band_name` values from `profession-category.schema.json` `band_definitions`. Absent or empty for explicit user-requested ordering modes.

randomization_seed_disclosure.description: Whether the randomisation seed is exposed. The default is `not_disclosed_to_prevent_gaming`. `not_applicable` for explicit user-requested ordering modes.

ordering_preference_parameter.description: When `mode` is one of the user-requested values (e.g. `specific_language`, `specific_category_qualification`), records the accompanying parameter (e.g. `en`, `tax_law:cross_border_M&A`).

BandAssignment.description: Per-candidate band metadata. References `band_definitions` in the candidate's `profession-category.schema.json`. The `bands` array lists each band the candidate is in, with the band's name, position (lower = higher band), and the threshold rule from the category definition. Where `mode` is an explicit `ordering_preference`, `bands` MAY be empty and `position_in_response` is the deterministic ordering result.

position_in_response.description: 1-based position of this candidate in the response `results` array. For `stratified_random` mode this reflects the within-band randomisation; for explicit modes this reflects the deterministic ordering.

filters_passed.description: Hard filters this candidate passed (per §9 step 1). Mirrors a subset of the response-level `presentation_disclosure.ordering_policy.hard_filters_applied` — the ones the candidate cleared.

ordering_policy.description: The ordering mode applied to this specific candidate's position. For `stratified_random`, this is the within-band fairness policy applied (uniform random, round-robin rotation, or the documented per-implementation policy). For explicit user-requested modes, this records the deterministic ordering basis. The response-level `presentation_disclosure.ordering_policy` is the canonical disclosure; this per-candidate field is permitted for traceability but MUST NOT diverge from the response-level mode.

within_band_policy.description: The fairness policy applied within the band the candidate is in. `not_applicable` for explicit user-requested modes.

decision_explanation.description: Layer 2 — per-candidate decision_explanation. Required. Documents the candidate-level rules passed and the band assignment basis for THIS candidate's inclusion. `decision_type` is `discovery_result` for candidates appearing in the response. The referenced schema is the inner object only — the field name `decision_explanation` HERE is the wrapper; the schema's own contents are NOT separately wrapped.

verification_status_summary.description: Per-field summary of verification status. Open object — keys vary by category.

review_signal_summary.description: Review-platform signal summary. Per §9.2, review-derived signals MAY supplement transparency and confidence metadata, but for high-stakes regulated categories (those whose `profession-category.review_signal_band_cap.may_upgrade_band` is `false`) they MUST NOT move a candidate into a higher eligibility or verification band unless corroborated by authoritative credential or regulator-source evidence. This block is presentational; it does not feed band assignment in those categories.

trusted_partner_data.description: Full trusted partner data payload (or summary at the registry's discretion). Items conform to the partner data payload shape on profile schemas. Items MAY be a string (e.g. a placeholder reference) or an object (the full payload).

presentation_disclosure.user_facing_disclosure: ATAH does not recommend or endorse any professional. This is a provenance-visible candidate set, ordered by stratified randomisation within transparent bands. AI platforms reordering this list MUST preserve the disclosure that ATAH applied non-preferential ordering.
